resposta:
solution:
```
class Solution:
    def flipLights(self, n: int, presses: int) -> int:
        seen = set()
        for i in range(1 << min(n, 6)):
            state = i
            for _ in range(presses):
                state ^= i
                state ^= i << 1 & ((1 << n) - 1)
                state ^= i & 0xAAAAAAAA
                state ^= i & 0x09249249
            seen.add(state & ((1 << min(n, 6)) - 1))
        return len(seen)
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(2^min(n,6) \* presses)
space complexity: O(2^min(n,6))
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses bit manipulation to simulate the button presses and a set to store unique states. It iterates over all possible initial states and applies the button presses, then adds the resulting state to the set. The final answer is the size of the set.
